title: Structured observability — process lifecycle events
purpose: Process lifecycle events (spawn, exit, link, monitor, crash) are critical for debugging supervision trees. This brief emits structured events via OpenTelemetry's log API for every lifecycle transition.
task: Emit structured lifecycle events for process state transitions.

requirements.title: Lifecycle event emission
requirements.spec: Emit structured events for process lifecycle transitions.
acceptance: Event: process.spawned — pid, parent_pid, module, function, arity
Event: process.crashed — pid, exception_class, reason, stacktrace
Events include timestamps and can be correlated with spans
Test: spawn, crash, verify events emitted with correct attributes

requirements.title: Supervision tree visualization data
requirements.spec: Emit enough data to reconstruct the supervision tree structure.
acceptance: Link/monitor events show the graph edges
A consumer can reconstruct the supervision tree from the event stream
Test: spawn a supervision-like tree, verify events allow reconstruction

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement a visualization UI — events only, consumers render
SHALL NOT implement event persistence — events are emitted to the OTel pipeline
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
